lexical_units.basic_meaning: weapons, a contest, absorption, or engrossing possession
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: the war and public attention are framed as combat and consuming attention
lexical_units.review_notes: Mostly conventional war-register wording; retain moderate confidence.
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political conflict is understood through combat and physical possession.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Public affairs and attention are construed through combat and physical absorption.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: tearing fabric/body, dissolving substance, or dividing an object
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: the Union is represented as a body or material object that can be torn, dissolved, or divided
lexical_units.review_notes: Union semantic-control note applies; keep legal and body/material readings distinct.
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political union is understood through material cohesion.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: The constitutional Union is abstract-political but construed as a separable material entity.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: tearing fabric/body, dissolving substance, or dividing an object

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a living organism continues alive, dies, perishes, survives, or endures bodily strain
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: the nation or government continues or ceases political existence
lexical_units.review_notes: Body-politic survival language; use as MIPVU evidence only with its local national subject.
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political survival is understood through organismic life and death.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: A polity is not a biological organism, but its political existence is construed as bodily life or death.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: twisting liquid out of something, bread as food, sweat on faces
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: enslaved labor is represented through extracting bread from bodily sweat
lexical_units.review_notes: Biblical/allusive phrase; the bodily labor is literal but 'wringing bread' is figurative.
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Slavery's labor economy is understood through bodily extraction.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: The expression frames exploitation through bodily extraction and biblical labor idiom.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: stumbling block, sin, calamity, divine government, or transfer/gift
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: slavery and war are framed through biblical offense, woe, providence, and divine giving
lexical_units.review_notes: Keep uncertain under the historical-semantics note for offense/providence.
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Historical causation may be understood through biblical moral accounting.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Biblical usage may be conventional theological idiom rather than a separable metaphor.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a whip or instrument of punishment; movement away from a place
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: war is represented as a divine or punitive scourge that passes away
lexical_units.review_notes: Providential punishment imagery; interpret with caution.
lexical_units.comparison_basis: War is understood through punitive bodily punishment.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: War is not literally a whip, but it is explicitly called a scourge.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: descended below a surface or into water/earth
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: wealth produced by enslaved labor is exhausted or consumed
lexical_units.review_notes: Part of moral-accounting sequence; may map with debt/accounting rather than body metaphor.
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Economic repayment or loss is understood through physical sinking/descent.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Wealth is not literally submerged; it is imagined as disappearing downward or being consumed.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: settle a debt by giving money or equivalent value
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: bloodshed is morally repaid by further bloodshed in war
lexical_units.review_notes: High-salience moral accounting unit; interpret only with blood/lash/sword and providential judgment context.
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Historical judgment is understood through debt repayment and moral accounting.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Blood is not currency, but violence is framed as repayment for prior violence.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a bladed weapon
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: war violence or armed force in the repayment phrase
lexical_units.review_notes: Preserve uncertainty: could be literal weapon, metonym for war, or biblical idiom. Do not use as high-confidence metaphor evidence without adjudication.
lexical_units.comparison_basis: War may be represented by its weapon, but this may be metonymic rather than metaphorical.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: The local phrase may refer to literal weapons, war by metonymy, or biblical/legal retribution imagery.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: legal decision or sentence by a judge
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: divine moral determinations concerning the war and slavery
lexical_units.review_notes: Requires historical semantics control before CMT mapping; record as uncertain rather than force legal-source mapping.
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Providential meaning may be understood through legal judgment, but period biblical idiom may make it conventional rather than metaphorical.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: The term is biblical and theological in context, but legal and divine judgment senses overlap historically.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: binding and tending bodily wounds
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: repairing the nation after war
lexical_units.review_notes: High-salience body-politic healing expression in the closing sentence.
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political reconciliation is understood through healing an injured body.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: The nation is treated as an injured body capable of wounds and care.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: hold, care for, or nurse a beloved living being
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: peace is treated as something to nurture and hold dear
lexical_units.review_notes: Closing reconciliation language; lower confidence because conventional.
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political peace is understood through care/nurture.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Peace is abstract but is construed as an object or living good to be cherished.
